title: Meet the AI-powered fan companion: TGR Haas F1 Team RaceMate powered by Infobip
article_text:
New always-on conversational agent with team race intelligence, team insights, and interactive experiences powered by Infobip AgentOS

Global AI-first cloud communications platform Infobip and TGR Haas F1 team are launching 'TGR Haas F1 Team RaceMate powered by Infobip', an AI-powered conversational fan companion on Apple Messages for Business and WhatsApp Business Platform. Always-on and always available, it delivers race intelligence for TGR Haas F1 Team: team race intelligence, grid positions, qualifying outcomes, sprint results, and full session schedules. The AI agent tracks drivers Ollie Bearman and Esteban Ocon with their individual championship standings and performance data in conversational format.

The agent draws from a knowledge base covering driver biographies, team history, and circuit data, adapting to each user's knowledge level. Every interaction begins with a schedule check, ensuring fans always know what's happening right now. It proactively surfaces relevant information, suggests next actions, and maintains conversational flow to keep every interaction effortless and engaging. Delivered in short, succinct messages with the energy of a fellow fan in the grandstand and with smart quick-reply menus, the experience is designed for modern fans seeking instant access to team insights.

Marketing Technology News: MarTech Interview with Stephen Howard-Sarin, MD of Retail Media, Americas @ Criteo

Between race weekends, the AI-powered solution keeps fans engaged through gamified experiences: team knowledge quizzes with leaderboards, trivia, and the ability to browse official TGR Haas F1 team merchandise with virtual try-on technology, all without leaving the conversation.

The conversational agent represents the evolution of the Infobip and TGR Haas F1 team partnership announced in Miami last year. Year one proved fan appetite for richer engagement through gamified activations. The foundational infrastructure for year two and beyond enables the partnership's core promise: deeper connections between team and its global fanbase. Built on Infobip's AgentOS technology, it scales to millions of fans while maintaining conversational quality and personalization.

"TGR Haas F1 Team RaceMate powered by Infobip transforms how we connect with our fanbase on their terms," said Michael Heath, Senior Fan Engagement & CRM Manager at TGR Haas F1 Team. "An always-on companion that gives fans instant access to the race schedule, team race intelligence, team insights, and personalized experiences. We're not just launching an agent; we're building a deeper relationship with the global community that loves this team."

Marketing Technology News: From MarTech Stack to MarTech Fabric: Weaving Brand, Content, and Conversion Into One Thread

"With this AI-powered fan companion, we're putting technology in service of genuine relationships and demonstrating the business potential of conversational AI in sports," said Ante Pamuković, Chief Revenue Officer at Infobip. "It provides what modern fans expect, driving loyalty, expanding fan reach and engagement depth in the digital era."

TGR Haas F1 Team RaceMate powered by Infobip represents a new model for how sports teams can build direct, personalized, always-available relationships with their global fanbase. The fan companion launches during the Miami Grand Prix taking place from 1-3 May 2026 and will be available throughout the 2026 season on WhatsApp Business Platform and Apple Messages for Business.

title: Applied Optoelectronics Inc. receives $20.85M grant to promote semiconductor manufacturing in Sugar Land
article_text:
Applied Optoelectronics Inc. is expanding its manufacturing facilities in Sugar Land. (Courtesy AOI)

Applied Optoelectronics Inc. will receive a $20.85 million grant for its manufacturing expansion in Sugar Land, Gov. Greg Abbott announced in an April 29 news release.

Founded in 1997, the domestic optical module manufacturer company designs and manufactures fiber-optic networking products, including semiconductor chips and transceivers used to build high-speed optical networking infrastructure, per the news release.

What it means

The grant -- which will be used to support AOI's new 266,000-square-foot facility and a new manufacturing line at its existing headquarters -- comes from the Texas Semiconductor Innovation Fund, a program that Abbott signed into law under the Texas CHIPS Act in 2023, according to the release.

The expansion, which was announced in July 2025, is expected to bring 500 jobs to the area.

The grant program was created to continue Texas's leadership in semiconductor research, design and manufacturing as well as "maintain the state's position as the nation's leader in semiconductor manufacturing," per the release.

"Texas is leading America's resurgence in advanced manufacturing," Abbott said. "This investment by AOI to expand their operations in Sugar Land will create hundreds of high-skilled jobs and advance our state's leadership in innovation and semiconductor manufacturing."

title: Applied Optoelectronics receives $20.9M Texas grant for expansion By Investing.com
article_text:
SUGAR LAND, Texas - Applied Optoelectronics Inc. (NASDAQ:AAOI) received a $20,852,518 grant from the Texas Semiconductor Innovation Fund to support manufacturing expansion in Sugar Land, Texas, according to a press release statement.

The company plans to occupy an additional 210,000-square-foot manufacturing facility adjacent to its current headquarters. The expansion aims to establish production capacity for data center transceivers and create over 500 jobs in the coming years. The announcement comes as AAOI's market capitalization has reached $11.55 billion, with shares delivering a remarkable 971% return over the past year.

The Texas Semiconductor Innovation Fund provides grants to higher education institutions, state entities, and businesses for research, development, and economic activities related to semiconductor design and manufacturing.

"The support from the State of Texas is critical to our expansion plans and is a tangible commitment by the state to advancing semiconductor innovation," said Stefan Murry, Chief Financial Officer and Chief Strategy Officer at Applied Optoelectronics.

Applied Optoelectronics develops and manufactures optical and HFC networking products for cloud computing, CATV broadband, telecom, and fiber-to-the-home markets. The company was founded by a team from the University of Houston 29 years ago and operates research and development facilities in Atlanta and manufacturing facilities in Sugar Land, Texas, as well as locations in Taipei, Taiwan and Ningbo, China. Analysts predict the company will be profitable this year with anticipated sales growth, according to InvestingPro, which currently rates the stock as overvalued relative to its Fair Value.

The company supplies infrastructure products to customers across cloud computing, CATV broadband, telecom, and FTTH markets.

In other recent news, Applied Optoelectronics Inc. announced it received a significant $71 million order for 800G single-mode data center transceivers from a major hyperscale customer. This development follows a previous $53 million order from the same customer, bringing the total orders to $124 million since mid-March. The company is expanding its manufacturing capacity in Texas, adding two adjacent buildings in Pearland, which will increase its footprint by approximately 388,000 square feet. This expansion complements its existing operations, including a 210,000-square-foot facility under development near its Sugar Land headquarters. Despite a short position disclosed by Citron Research, Applied Optoelectronics' stock has seen a rise, with the optical components sector benefiting from the AI infrastructure build-out. The company's recent activities highlight its strategic focus on meeting the growing demand for data center transceivers, which are crucial for AI-driven workloads.

title: Burundi Moves Ahead with $5.9 Million Rural 4G Expansion
article_text:
Initiative aims to narrow digital divide, boost connectivity

Burundi's state-owned telecom operator Onatel signed a $5.9 million contract on Wednesday with PAFEN Burundi to deploy 4G mobile internet across underserved rural communities, under the oversight of the Ministry of Finance, Budget and Digital Economy.

Backed by the World Bank, the project aims to connect 92 localities and reach more than 370,000 people within 18 months. The initiative is designed to reduce the digital divide between urban centers and rural areas.

The contract is the latest in a series of investments in digital infrastructure. On March 2, 2026, operator Lumitel Burundi signed a separate $10 million agreement with Burundian authorities, also under the PAFEN framework, to expand 4G coverage in rural areas. Together, the two deals form part of a broader program targeting connectivity for 178 localities, reaching nearly 786,000 residents.

Despite this momentum, Burundi faces a significant connectivity gap. According to the International Telecommunication Union, nearly 90% of the population lacked internet access in 2023. Recent national data show improvement, with internet penetration estimated at 26% in 2024, driven mainly by mobile access.

However, disparities remain stark. 3G coverage reaches about 50.6% of the population, while 4G covers only 32.18%. 2G remains the dominant technology, particularly in rural areas where some localities still lack reliable connectivity. In 2023, around 67.8% of the population had no 4G coverage, compared with 46.8% without 3G.

To address this, authorities are pursuing a public digital infrastructure model based on open access and national roaming, allowing all operators to use the networks being deployed. The project also plans to incorporate renewable energy sources, notably solar power, to address low electrification rates in rural areas.

Infrastructure alone will not remove all barriers to adoption. The cost of compatible handsets, the affordability of data packages and low levels of digital literacy remain key constraints. Authorities say expanding broadband access is a necessary foundation for developing digital services such as e-government, digital health, online education and electronic payments.

title: MTN Nigeria posts N355.5bn profit, flags diesel costs as key risk
article_text:
MTN Nigeria reported a profit after tax of N355.5bn for the first quarter of 2026, representing a 165.9 per cent increase compared to the same period last year, but warned that rising diesel prices could pressure earnings in the months ahead.

In its unaudited financial results released on Wednesday, the telecom operator projected a potential decline of between 1.8 and 2.0 percentage points in its full-year Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation and Amortisation (EBITDA) margin if diesel prices average N2,000 per litre in the second half of the year.

With about 89.5 million subscribers, MTN Nigeria operates over 20,000 base stations nationwide, the majority of which rely on diesel-powered generators due to persistent instability in the national power grid.

Chief Executive Officer Karl Toriola said the company remains cautious about developments in the operating environment, particularly fluctuations in energy costs and regulatory conditions.

The outlook reflects growing uncertainty in Nigeria's fuel market, driven by global crude oil disruptions and local supply challenges. In March, geopolitical tensions involving the United States, Israel and Iran disrupted activity around the Strait of Hormuz, pushing crude oil prices above $100 per barrel and increasing global fuel import costs.

These pressures have filtered into Nigeria's deregulated downstream sector, resulting in higher fuel prices nationwide. The Dangote Refinery recently adjusted its diesel price to about N1,750 per litre, while prices at some independent stations have risen to roughly N1,250 per litre in parts of the country.

MTN Nigeria noted that if diesel prices in Lagos average N2,000 per litre in the second half of the year, the impact on profitability could be significant, with EBITDA margins expected to weaken accordingly.

According to the State of Africa's Infrastructure Report 2025 by the Africa Finance Corporation, telecom operators in Nigeria consume more than 40 million litres of diesel monthly to power network infrastructure. This equates to over 480 million litres annually, with industry spending estimated at more than $350m.

Meanwhile, MTN Nigeria significantly increased its capital expenditure during the quarter, with spending (excluding right-of-use assets) rising by 92.8 per cent year-on-year to N390.3bn from N202.4bn in Q1 2025.

The company said much of the investment was directed toward expanding network capacity and enhancing broadband services, including fibre-to-the-home deployment and fixed wireless access infrastructure.

title: Sinclair Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
article_text:
BALTIMORE, April 30, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Sinclair, Inc. (Nasdaq: SBGI), the "Company" or "Sinclair," today reported financial results for the three months ended March 31, 2026.

Highlights:

* Total Revenue increased by 4% and Total Adjusted EBITDA by 13% year-over-year

* Total Adjusted EBITDA of $126 million

* Strong core advertising performance driven by growth in digital

* March 2026 was Tennis Channel's most-watched month ever

* Stable distribution trend due to moderating churn across key MVPDs

* Reaffirmed 2026 full year financial guidance

CEO Comment:

"Sinclair continues to execute on its core broadcast business, with both ratings and subscriber trends showing positive momentum. Broadcast's reach differentiation continues to drive record viewing levels continuing into a political- and sports-heavy 2026 for the industry. Live sports remained a key driver in the quarter, with the Super Bowl in February delivering the second-largest audience in U.S. television history and the Winter Olympics also delivering record viewing levels. Tennis Channel had its most-watched month ever in March, including four of the top-five most-watched matches in network history, and delivered record subscriber numbers for its Direct-to-Consumer product. Based on our first quarter results and current outlook, we are reaffirming our 2026 full year financial guidance."

Recent Developments:

Balance Sheet

* Retired $165 million of term loans at a discount in early April through an unmodified reverse Dutch auction, which will save approximately $12 million in annual interest expense.

* Ended the first quarter with total liquidity of ~$1.5 billion, including cash of $844 million.

Investment Portfolio

* Sinclair Ventures, LLC (Ventures) received distributions of approximately $12 million and ended the quarter with $451 million in cash.

Station Portfolio Optimization

* Closed substantial majority of our partner station acquisitions and continue to expect $30 million in annualized synergies in 2026.

Content and Distribution

* NBC affiliates delivered strong results in the first quarter as the Super Bowl was the 2nd-most watched U.S. telecast of all-time while the Winter Olympics was the most-watched Winter Games since 2014.

* Tennis Channel delivered its two most-watched women's matches of all-time in March and four of its top five most watched matches ever.

title: CT Interactive expands portfolio, boosts Bulgaria presence in April | Yogonet International
article_text:
CT Interactive reported steady growth in April, driven by new game launches, expansion in its home market, and participation in industry events.

The company introduced two new slot titles during the month, including 10 Blazing Treasures and 100 Burning Brilliants, as it continues to broaden its gaming portfolio. The releases are aimed at enhancing player engagement through a mix of modern mechanics and classic gameplay features.

CT Interactive also strengthened its position in Bulgaria by adding 20 new titles to its local offering, expanding content available to partners in the market.

On the events front, the company took part in SBC Summit Malta 2026 as a supporting sponsor, with Chief Operating Officer Martin Ivanov and Chief Commercial Officer Monika Zlateva attending to meet partners and explore business opportunities.

Zlateva also spoke at the event on industry trends, presenting on the topic "Media and Messaging: From Sales to Storytelling in a Restricted Age".

"April's achievements reflect CT Interactive's ongoing focus on quality and strategic partnerships," the supplier said. "The company remains committed to expanding its reach and delivering exceptional gaming experiences across global markets."

title: How to watch Louisiana softball vs South Alabama in final SBC series
article_text:
Louisiana softball is climbing the SBC standings and its series win against Marshall was key. Here's what HC Alyson Habetz had to say on the series.

Louisiana softball has just one more Sun Belt Conference series ahead of it before the Cajuns will return to Lamson Park to host the 2026 SBC Softball Championship Tournament.

Last time out, the Ragin' Cajuns (27-24, 10-11) handled business against the No. 1 team in the SBC standings. They took two out of three games against Marshall in the comforts of home, helping them climb up the standings. This week, another chance to secure a higher seeding is on the tap for the Cajuns as they hit the road for the final time to take on South Alabama.

The Jaguars (27-24,11-10) are currently a game ahead of UL in the SBC standings after getting swept by Southern Mississippi last week. South Alabama's last game against the Golden Eagles only lasted six innings as the Jaguars were run-ruled in an 8-0 loss. The chip on the Jaguars' shoulders is even heavier heading into this series as the Cajuns have had their number for quite some time now.

UL hasn't lost to South Alabama since 2015, currently on a 29-game winning streak over the Jaguars. Second-year head coach Alyson Habetz and the Cajuns will look to keep their winning streak alive and shake their road trip woes that they've had this season. They hold a 2-15 away record entering the series, struggling to get the job done away from home.

title: Little Caesars Pizza AI-enables phone order management
article_text:
The world's third-largest pizza chain is expanding its efforts to streamline the processing of customer phone orders.

Following its successful deployment in corporate stores, Little Caesars Pizza is deploying the GoToConnect artificial intelligence-based cloud communications platform from GoTo in franchise locations. The retailer began deploying GoToConnect, which automates the answering and handling of customer phone orders, at corporate restaurants nationwide in 2017.

Little Caesars consolidates its store communications tech stack on GoToConnect and is able t0 quickly onboard new stores with cloud-based phone system setup which it says minimizes on-site repairs and maintenance.

In addition, the company gains access to call analytics to track trends, performance, and opportunities across locations and says by automating phone orders it frees up store associates to focus on providing in-person customer service.

"We began by rolling out GoTo Connect in our corporate restaurants and then expanded to franchises, and it has transformed how guests connected with our restaurants," said Lance Shinabarger, CIO, shared technologies and applications, Little Caesars. "We're successfully handling millions of calls a month. We've delivered a better customer experience and reduced costs at the same time."

title: Powerful and compact SBC from MSI sports up to 32 GB DDR5 RAM and 8-core Intel CPU
article_text:
MSI has introduced a brand-new high-performance SBC (single-board computer), dubbed the MS-CF27. With up to 8-core Intel CPU and 32 GB of RAM, the 3.5-inch SBC offers a healthy set of features.

MSI has introduced a new 3.5" SBC with Intel's Alder Lake-N, Twin Lake, and Amston Lake CPUs. The SBC also offers a whole bunch of useful I/O, which is to be expected from a product aimed primarily at enterprise and hobbyist usage.

The MS-CF27 SBC offers 4-core and 8-core Intel CPU options

The SBC can be equipped with up to an 8-core Intel Core i3 N305 CPU. Lower-end 4-core CPU options are also available, namely the N97, N150, and Atom x7433RE CPUs. The Core i3 N305 is no slouch, and can easily keep up with older laptop CPUs such as the Core i7 11390H in multicore performance.

For moderately demanding tasks, the 8-core variant of the MS-CF27 SBC should perform well enough. The lower-end 4-core variants should only be good enough for lightweight tasks.

title: BMC's updated Kaius gravel race bike gets more off-road-friendly while adding Teammachine aerodynamics
article_text:
BMC's updated gravel race bike moves with the times, with clearance for 52mm tyres, less aggressive geometry and more mounts, plus aero improvements taken from the Teammachine R road bike

BMC says that gravel racing has evolved, with riders demanding aero features, handling for riding at high speeds, low weight and capability over a variety of surfaces. In response, the new BMC Kaius gravel race bike has 52mm tyre clearance, updated geometry, a 62g lower frame weight and a claimed 12% improvement in aerodynamics.

The three available specs are all equipped with SRAM XPLR AXS groupsets and cost from €5,499 to €10,999; there's also a frameset option.

When the original Kaius was launched in 2022, gravel race bikes typically had road-adjacent geometry and tyre clearance, both more aggressive than many general purpose gravel bikes. The Kaius came from this mould and was ridden to victory in the inaugural UCI Gravel World Championship by Pauline Ferrand-Prévot that year.

But the view on the optimum setup for gravel racing has evolved since then, with larger tyre clearance close to 50mm typical, along with more storage options and, in many cases, suspension-adjusted steering.

According to Stefan Christ, BMC's head of R&D, 'The original Kaius received exceptional rider feedback, even at a time when gravel racing was still emerging as a category. Retaining its confidence-inspiring ride feel while refining performance and tailoring it to the needs of today's riders and courses was the core challenge behind its evolution.'

Thus the new BMC Kaius 01 aims to address many of the changing trends since the original was launched, while reducing the frame weight by a claimed 63g. BMC says that a size 54 frameset weighs 998g, while the wide stance Halo fork, based on that of the Teammachine R aero road bike, weighs 400g. It claims a 7.1kg full bike weight in top One spec.

BMC has also reshaped the bike's aero profiles, basing them on the Teammachine R, resulting in a claimed 12% drag reduction. The bike is claimed to be faster with bottles than without and comes fitted with the same AeroCore bottle cages as BMC's road bikes.

There's a third bottle cage mount under the down tube as well as rivets above and below the top tube, but there isn't in-frame storage.

At the same time, tyre clearance has been increased to 52mm from 44mm, although BMC says you can fit tyres as narrow as 32mm without affecting the new bike's handling. The frame is designed for wireless 1x groupsets, with the option to fit a chainring from 32T to 50T with an integrated chainguide.

At the front end, the bike's stack has been increased by 11mm and the reach decreased by 5mm. There's the same head tube angle of around 71°, dependent on frame size, as on the previous Kaius, but trail has been increased to 72mm, 4mm longer than the old model.

BMC has kept the same bottom bracket drop, but on the larger tyres the ground clearance has increased by 10mm. The seat tube angle also varies by frame size, while chainstay length has been increased by just 5mm to accommodate the extra tyre clearance.

BMC claims that together these changes allow an aggressive riding position, with stable and confidence-inspiring handling.

The brand says that the frame compliance is identical to the previous generation bike, although the seat tube has been engineered to add extra deflection and is paired with the 134g AS10 aero seatpost used on the Teammachine SLR 01. The bike comes with the new, narrow ICS Carbon Aero integrated cockpit, which has a 12° flare and supports internal cabling and is also fitted to top specs of the Teammachine SLR and Teammachine R.

Clara Koppenburg, who rides for Tudor Pro Cycling's gravel team, says, 'The Kaius has always been an absolute standout. I've never ridden a bike that felt so confident and secure on the descents while still being incredibly fast on the climbs and flats. It's a bike that forgives small steering mistakes and lets you stay focused on racing.'

BMC will sell the new Kaius in three specs and as a frameset and in sizes 47, 51, 54, 56, 58 and 61cm.

title: Airtel eyes improved network capacity with new sites
source_name: Latest Nigeria News, Nigerian Newspapers, Politics
article_text:
Airtel Nigeria said it is targeting improved network capacity and ultimately improved quality of service (QoS) to end users of telecom services, according to its Chief Executive Officer, Dinesh Balsingh.

"Over the last two years we have expanded our geographical network footprint by over 10 percent, and we keep going forward. This expansion allows us to improve coverage, strengthen capacity and ensure that customers receive a reliable digital experience wherever they are," he said.

Across the country, the mobile signal that powers daily life travels through a vast lattice of antennas, towers and fibre cables stretching across cities, highways and rural communities. The infrastructure behind those signals rarely attracts attention, yet it is expanding at a remarkable pace.

According to him, over the past three years, the telco has significantly increased the scale of its network, and, in February this year, it has grown the number of sites to more than 17,000.

Between 2023 and 2025, the telecom operator had added over 2,000 new sites, expanding its network footprint by about 15.5 percent and bringing the total number of operational sites to roughly 16,711 nationwide, according to company disclosures reported by industry publications.

Why men fear court marriages, by Ubi Franklin

In an industry already defined by large infrastructure investments, the expansion stands out. It places Airtel among the fastest-growing networks in Nigeria by site growth over the period.

The expansion is happening against the backdrop of rising digital demand across the country.

Data from the Nigerian Communications Commission (NCC) shows that the number of base stations nationwide increased from 127,294 in 2022 to 137,992 in 2023, reflecting the industry's ongoing effort to strengthen connectivity capacity.

By the end of 2024, according to the NCC 2024 Year-End Performance Report, the total number of BTS across Nigeria had climbed to 145,141, highlighting the scale of infrastructure required to support the country's fast-growing digital economy.

This tally captures layers of 2G, 3G, 4G and 5G installed nationwide on telecom cell sites, enabling operators to push more bandwidth through the same physical footprint.

Of this number of base stations, 46,918 belong to Airtel.

The push to expand telecom infrastructure reflects the central role connectivity now plays in the economy.

"The accurate measure of connectivity is the economic value it creates," Executive Vice Chairman of the NCC, Aminu Maida, said.

These BTS form the backbone of mobile communications in Africa's largest telecom market. Nigeria now has more than 129 million internet users, the vast majority of whom access digital services through mobile networks, citing Telecommunications in Nigeria industry statistics.

Every additional smartphone increases the pressure on existing networks.

Calls, video streaming, digital payments and online services all compete for bandwidth within the same infrastructure. When capacity falls behind demand, users experience slower speeds or dropped connections.

Telecom operators therefore face a constant task: expand the network before congestion becomes visible to customers.

For Airtel Nigeria, the latest expansion is part of a broader investment cycle aimed at strengthening both coverage and capacity.

The new sites are being deployed across a range of environments, from dense urban neighbourhoods to smaller towns where network coverage has historically been thinner.

Industry analysts say this two-front strategy reflects the realities of Nigeria's telecom market.

Cities require additional towers to handle the enormous volume of data traffic generated by millions of connected devices. Rural areas require entirely new infrastructure to close coverage gaps.

The technical planning behind these deployments falls to Airtel Nigeria's Chief Technology Officer (CTO), Harmanpreet Singh Dhillon, who oversees network engineering and capacity management.

"Network demand is constantly evolving. As more Nigerians come online, the network must grow ahead of that demand so customers can continue to enjoy consistent connectivity," he said.

Beyond building new sites, Airtel has also focused on upgrading existing infrastructure.

The company says nearly 99 per cent of its sites now support high-speed 4G broadband, extending fast internet access across much of its network footprint.

In the past year alone, more than 5,000 locations received capacity upgrades, improving network performance and strengthening reliability during periods of heavy traffic.

Behind these technical upgrades lies a broader shift in the economics of telecom networks.

A decade ago, mobile infrastructure was designed primarily to support voice calls. Today the dominant driver is data. Streaming video, cloud services, mobile banking and social media all require far greater bandwidth than traditional voice traffic.

Nigeria's telecom operators are therefore investing heavily in both towers and fibre backhaul, the high-capacity cables that carry data between base stations and core networks.

Airtel says it is also expanding its fibre backbone by about 25 percent, enabling greater capacity across its network and improving resilience during traffic surges.

The broader industry remains intensely competitive.

MTN Nigeria continues to operate the country's largest telecom network by subscriber base and infrastructure scale. But the pace of infrastructure expansion is becoming an increasingly important metric of competitiveness as data consumption accelerates.

For consumers, the impact of network expansion is often felt in small but meaningful ways. Web pages load faster. Video calls remain stable. Banking apps complete transactions without interruption.

Across Nigeria's landscape, with every new tower that rises above markets, neighbourhoods and highways, the reach of the country's digital economy is expected to extend further.

With thousands of new sites added in only two years, a telecom company such as Airtel may be steadily expanding its place within that infrastructure map.

title: Jobs Connect opens global pathways and builds future skills for Cape Town residents
article_text:
Mayoral Committee Member for Economic Growth, Alderman James Vos, says the City of Cape Town's Jobs Connect platform is expanding opportunities for residents, as the programme reaches two important milestones in international placement and youth skills development. A group of 10 Capetonians recruited and trained through the platform arrived in Germany to start their bus driver jobs, and a group of 50 learners received guidance on pursuing careers in the Information and Communication Technology industry. Read more below:

The bus drivers are beneficiaries of Jobs Connect's first international recruitment drive and saw selected candidates receive thorough training and assessments.

'Creating employment pathways for Capetonians on the international stage is an exciting new development for Jobs Connect. I also want to thank officials in the German Consulate for their support with work visas and relocation logistics. The platform is now advertising further opportunities for individuals in other fields who are willing to relocate to Germany,' said Alderman Vos.

Qualified Mechatronics Technicians or Engineers are encouraged to apply for posts based in Germany via this link. Applications close on 1 June 2026.

Currently, 929 positions are advertised on Jobs Connect for Cape Town-based opportunities, spanning various industries and experience levels. Registration, assessment and job applications are free of charge, with the platform also being data cost-free for MTN and Vodacom users. Candidates selected for interviews are automatically notified via SMS or WhatsApp.

'From the outset, Jobs Connect was about showing that innovation in government doesn't have to be complicated, it can be simple and impactful when you clearly identify a problem and design a solution that directly addresses it. In this case, that meant reducing the barriers to employment while helping residents access real opportunities. Since it went live in early 2021, 170 000 people have accessed Jobs Connect and used it to build their employment profiles. I've had the fortune of joining the team at activations and helping first-time job-seekers to navigate the system, which includes in-depth numeracy and literacy assessments and detailed guidance on developing CVs that they use for work applications on Jobs Connect and other platforms. I'm encouraged by the results we've seen so far, but we know there is still more to do. We will keep building on this momentum because the programme is making a tangible difference,' said Alderman Vos.

Between July 2023 and April 2026, 12 914 people secured employment (including 432 graduates and 388 people with disabilities) and 2 376 accessed education opportunities.

'The reality is that, in South Africa, job-hunting is expensive. A 2023 survey from Youth Capital found that respondents spent an average of almost R1 500 on transport, data and applications. Jobs Connect helps reduce that burden through a streamlined, user-friendly and cost-effective system,' said Alderman Vos.

This past week, Alderman Vos also participated in the International Girls in ICT Day event hosted by the Jobs Connect team together with tech skills development organisations, Go Fourth International, Cisco Networking Academy, and the I-CAN Centre. The event engaged Grade 11 and 12 learners from Elsies River High School, focusing on ICT career pathways and mindset development.

'It was encouraging to see young learners building their awareness and readiness for a rapidly evolving industry. If we are serious about building an inclusive and future-ready economy, we must ensure that young people, regardless of their background, have access to the right tools and information. Through initiatives like Jobs Connect, we are turning interest into opportunity,' said Alderman Vos.

How to get started on Jobs Connect

To get onto Jobs Connect, work-seekers need to register their details on the website, https://jobsconnect.leelyn.co.za/ and complete a literacy and numeracy assessment.

Business owners looking for talent can also register as an employer by contacting Leelyn Management at info@leelyn.co.za or phone 021 558 1552.

title: Running local AI on the Raspberry Pi 5 taught me why cloud models are still winning
article_text:
Charles has worked with many technologies, from Arduino to PCs, over the past 10 years. He's interested in anything electronic, but especially the development of software and video games.

He has a passion for writing, playing video games, programming, singing in the shower, and spending time with friends.

Except for Google's AI responses to searches, I've never used AI before, not once. Most people I know, when they hear this, say, "You haven't used AI? But it's so useful!" I disagreed; I'd seen the results Google's AI gave, and it honestly seemed overhyped. Then I thought about it and realized I couldn't really judge it without testing it. However, being into tech, I didn't want to risk my privacy, knowing the impact that can have, so I wanted to install the AIs locally instead.

I started self-hosting LLMs and absolutely loved it

Who needs OpenAI when your home lab can do the thinking for you?

Posts 13

By Raghav Sethi

However, finding a device to install them on seemed a bit tricky. I have a PC, but it's very old and doesn't have a very good GPU. And while I could've used that, its power efficiency is terrible. Then I remembered I have a Raspberry Pi 5 lying around, which would be perfect. So I looked up how to install AI on it. It turns out it was pretty easy. Here's how I did it, along with the pros and cons of using AI on the Raspberry Pi 5.

Installing Ollama was the easy part

Ollama was the easiest AI platform to install

From my research, Ollama would be the easiest AI platform to install on my Raspberry Pi 5, so that's the option I chose. The installation was fairly straightforward. I already had the Pi-Apps app store installed, so all I had to do was install Ollama from the menus. I started installing it, and overall, it was quick and painless! However, there was a problem: my SD Card was 32GB, and about 16GB was already taken up by Raspberry Pi OS, which meant the Ollama GUI installer couldn't install the models it came with.

This wasn't a big deal, however, as installing models with Ollama is relatively easy. I opened a terminal and ran "ollama pull " followed by the model name found on the Ollama site. Then it took about 3-4 hours to download the 3 models I chose, since I downloaded them concurrently, and I was ready to roll! I didn't know this, but I could've also used the GUI to install a model. The 3 models I chose were all ones I've heard of before: Llama, Gemma, and Deepseek. I opted to install the smallest version of each model, as I only had 32GB of space on the SD Card.

Using LLMs on the SBC was responsive

The AIs were very quick and didn't take long to boot

Starting with Deepseek-R1, it took about a minute to start, and about 30 seconds after that to finish. Llama3.2 took about 5-10 seconds to start, and 30 seconds to finish. Gemma 3, the fastest, took about 2-3 seconds to start writing and about 30 seconds to finish. This was really fast, even compared to the time it'd take me to search on Google and sift through responses. And that's coming from someone who generally gets correct results in the first non-sponsored spot on the first try.

It was a shocking experience to see a result come so quickly that I immediately assumed it was wrong. And that's the catch with the fast response times. Some of the answers were right: All of the models got the abbreviations "IT" and "CISSP" correct. This was genuinely impressive to me, as if it could answer this correctly, surely it could answer other basic questions correctly too.

But don't try to push them too hard

The AIs were wrong more often than not, even about basic things

However, the more I looked into the responses, the worse it looked. When asked, "What does the fox say?", none of the models knew I was referring to the 2013 Ylvis meme song. Worse, when asked about Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventure, none of the models, except Llama, knew what I was referring to. And, Llama mixed up the actors of Bill and Ted, among other things.

Worse yet, when asked who the 60th president of the United States was, they all hallucinated that it was Joe Biden, except Deepseek, who assumed it was William McKinley. Which doesn't really make a lot of sense; it thought the 60th president would be in 1841, a far cry from 2025, when the 47th and current president was elected. Even if every president had two non-consecutive terms, the 60th president would still be in 2029, well after 1841.

And, only Llama could tell that "The old man the boat, " is a proper sentence, when asked for the imaginary grammatical error. While it may sound incorrect, it refers to elderly people manning a boat. Deepseek assumed the old man and the boat were two separate objects, while Gemma assumed, even more confusingly, that the old man was the boat. While this sentence may seem confusing, I certainly don't think either of those interpretations would make sense to a human.

Raspberry Pi 5

CPU Arm Cortex-A76 (quad-core, 2.4GHz)

Memory Up to 8GB LPDDR4X SDRAM

Operating System Raspberry Pi OS (official)

Ports 2× USB 3.0, 2× USB 2.0, Ethernet, 2x micro HDMI, 2× 4-lane MIPI transceivers, PCIe Gen 2.0 interface, USB-C, 40-pin GPIO header

GPU VideoCore VII

Starting Price $60

The Raspberry Pi 5 is a cheap SBC that supports running basic AIs. It has a budget mobile SOC, the BCM2712, and 2-16GB of RAM.

See at Adafruit See at CanaKit

Expand Collapse

AI on the Raspberry Pi 5 is decent, if you know what you're doing

And that's the thing: I'm expecting human-level quality from an AI that has only 1 billion parameters. Yes, I think the future of AI is on embedded devices, running locally on your network. Yes, I think that is how AI will survive, after it runs out of steam. However, I don't think it's fair to judge it as that ideal, given where it is now. Right now, AI is designed to run in the cloud. Running it locally is cool, but it's not fully there yet. Once the technology improves, however, I could really see running it locally taking off. And I'm sure that's where we're headed.

Ollama is still the easiest way to start local LLMs, but it's the worst way to keep running them

Ollama is great for getting you started... just don't stick around.

Posts 11

By Adam Conway

I think if I had more experience, I'd be able to tell which AIs are worth using in local setups, as I can already see differences in the areas of expertise of some models. But I can't expect the AIs to be ready in every area yet; they're still in their infancy. The companies behind them are taking people's IP without permission. They are losing money running the tiers that they are. But once they become stable, it will be like the Rabbt R1, where it's a local model, running on your phone or a small network-connected box in your bedroom. It will be available without a subscription; instead, you pay for it and own it. If the hardware breaks, you can replace it, repair it yourself, or go to the vendor. I think that is what the future holds, but as of now, AI isn't ready for me. If you have a Pi 5 lying around and want to try AI on it, I highly recommend it; it was an eye-opening experience for me.

title: MTN profit hits N355.5bn, as diesel cost threatens outlook
article_text:
MTN Nigeria reported a profit after tax of N355.5bn in the first quarter of 2026, up 165.9 per cent year-on-year, but warned that higher energy prices could weigh on earnings in the coming quarters.

In its unaudited results released on Wednesday, the telecom operator said it expects a 1.8 to 2.0 percentage point decline in full-year Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation and Amortisation margins if diesel prices average N2,000 per litre in the second half of the year.

The country's biggest telco with 89.5 million subscribers, operates more than 20,000 base stations nationwide, most of which depend on diesel generators because of unreliable grid power.

"We continue to monitor developments in the operating environment, including energy price volatility and regulatory dynamics," Chief Executive Officer Karl Toriola said in the Q1 report.

The concern comes amid increasing volatility in Nigeria's fuel market following global crude oil disruptions and domestic supply pressures.

In March, tensions involving the United States, Israel and Iran disrupted activities around the Strait of Hormuz, pushing crude prices above $100 per barrel and raising fuel import costs globally.

The impact filtered into Nigeria's deregulated downstream sector, leading to higher pump prices across the country. The $20bn Dangote Refinery has increased its diesel price to N1750 per litre from N1800, while fuel prices at independent stations reportedly climbed to about N1,250 per litre in some states.

"Based on an assumed average Lagos ex-depot diesel price of N2,000 in H2, we estimate a 1.8-2.0 percentage point impact on full-year EBITDA margin," the executive added.

According to the State of Africa's Infrastructure Report 2025 by the Africa Finance Corporation, Nigerian telecom operators consume over 40 million litres of diesel every month to power base stations nationwide. This reliance is driven by persistent grid instability, which has made self-generation the default energy source for network operations.

On an annual basis, this translates to more than 480 million litres of diesel consumed by the sector, with industry estimates suggesting annual spending exceeding $350m.

MTN also ramped up investment spending during the period, with capital expenditure (excluding right-of-use assets) rising 92.8 per cent year-on-year to N390.3bn from N202.4bn in Q1 2025.

The company said a substantial portion of the investment was directed toward expanding network capacity and strengthening its fixed broadband footprint, particularly through fibre-to-the-home rollout and fixed wireless access infrastructure.

title: Northern Virginia Gay News - Falls Church News-Press Online
article_text:
House GOP Tries Again to Pass National "Don't Say LGBTQ" Bill

Pro-equality forces are mobilized this week to voice strong opposition to H.R. 2616, the federal so-called 'Stopping Indoctrination and Protecting Kids Act', a bill that would strip schools of federal funds for even acknowledging the existence of transgender people.

H.R. 2616 would ban schools across the country from teaching about gender identity, having books with transgender characters, or using the correct names and pronouns for transgender students.

Trans students deserve to feel safe and welcome in school. Policies like these that denigrate them just make their lives immeasurably harder, advocates insist. "Transgender students exist and deserve to live their lives with dignity. H.R. 2616 would make their lives immeasurably harder," according to a statement from the D.C.-based Human Rights Campaign. "Schools have a legal and moral duty to keep all students safe, and the data is clear: bills like this fail that test. They target kids for harassment and exclusion, put them at risk at home and in school, and tell teachers what they can and cannot teach. Beyond being completely out of touch with the priorities of the American public, it's a deeply cynical - and harmful - piece of legislation."

Section 2 of the bill would require parental consent before schools may update a student's gender designation on records or allow bathroom/locker room access consistent with their gender identity. Schools that fail to comply lose federal ESEA funding.

According to the HRC, "for trans students whose families are unsupportive or unsafe, this would strip away one of the few spaces where they may have felt affirmed. It forces schools to out students to their parents if the student takes certain steps to affirm their identity, putting some youth in impossible and sometimes dangerous situations."

Online Panel Discusses History of Gay Newspapers

Last weekend, the LGBTQ History Project led by August Bernardicou hosted an online panel of veterans of the earliest Stonewall era newspapers that arose to advance the equality movement. Participants included Martha Shelley of the Come Out! newspaper in New York, Allen Young, Perry Brass, Kevin McGirr of the Boston Fag Rag, and Gary Alinder of the San Francisco area Gay Sunshine.

After the Stonewall riots in June 1969, gays across the U.S. in an amazingly fast period of time arose to affirm their identities, ending eons of living double lives they affirmed a shared concern to stand against racism, for feminist goals, against the war in Vietnam, and for more progressive domestic policies, the panelists noted, which made identifying with something broader than gay rights, alone, a clear choice, thus forging the Gay Liberation Front movement.

Brass noted resistance to this came from some traditional closeted gay groups who wanted to perpetuate a "private club" approach to their lives. In New York, repression of gay gatherings and clubs had been in effect since 1964 when the New York mayor ordered gay bars closed for the World's Fair there that year.

Shelley said her group's Come Out newspaper said it all in its title, alone, and with a 4,000 circulation was republished and quoted worldwide. "Out of the closet and into the streets," was the radical political cry of that period.

Alinder said that in the Bay Area, the movement dovetailed with wider causes for social change, the name of the Gay Sunshine being a takeoff from the wider consciousness-expanding movement of that era.

Gay newspapers were needed because a lot of the so-called underground press of that era were bastions of sexism and racism. Even so, it was the coverage of gay protests in the Berkeley Barb alternative newspaper in the years leading up to Stonewall that began the elevation of wider awareness of the movement.

-Nicholas F. Benton

Christopher Street Magazine Feted at Gay Book Awards

Tom Steele, Chuck Ortleb, Patrick Merla, and Michael Denneny were presented with the Publishing Triangle Leadership award last week for their work on the groundbreaking literary magazine Christopher Street.

The 38th annual Publishing Triangle awards were given out at the New School honoring writers, editors, agents, publishers, and readers recognizing achievement across the breadth of LGBTQ literature.

Founded in 1988, the Publishing Triangle presented its first awards in 1989 and has since worked to promote LGBTQ+ literature and support the LGBTQ+ publishing community. Through its annual awards, public programs, and networking opportunities, the organization fosters greater recognition, visibility, and appreciation for LGBTQ+ voices and stories.

Will TV Ratings Reflect LGBTQ Content? Inquiry Begins

Federal Communications Commission (FCC) Chairman Brendan Carr has announced a public inquiry into whether television ratings should be modified to specifically flag LGBTQ+ content.

The Gay and Lesbian Association Against Discrimination (GLAAD) has noted that 23 percent of Americans under 30 identify as LGBTQ+ and that "more than 5 million children have LGBTQ+ parents," calling the move government overreach designed to "reshape culture, limit storytelling, and undermine free expression."

Russian Court Designates LGBTQ Publication 'Extremist'

A court in Russia's Oryol region declared Parni+, an LGBTQ+ publication active for nearly 18 years, an extremist organization -- meaning its organizers could face prison and are legally required to cease operations.

The outlet's Telegram channel has more than 30,000 subscribers. Editor-in-Chief Yevgeny Pisemsky, already on Russia's "foreign agents" list since 2020, said the ruling amounted to an admission that "the truth about LGBT people is more dangerous to the state than any propaganda."

The ruling follows the designation of gay journalist Vadim Vaganov as an individual "extremist" -- the first such designation for LGBTQ activism specifically.

title: Essential Considerations for Selecting a Phone System in Medical Offices
article_text:
Communication plays a pivotal role in ensuring efficient operations and quality patient care. One of the most significant components of communication within medical offices is the phone system. Selecting the right phone system can streamline operations, improve patient interactions, and enhance overall service delivery. Here are essential considerations for medical offices when choosing a phone system.

Understanding the Unique Needs of a Medical Office

Medical offices operate differently from other businesses, with unique requirements that must be considered when selecting a phone system. First, understand the volume of calls the office typically receives, including patient inquiries, appointment scheduling, and follow-ups. Medical offices often require multiple lines to manage these calls effectively. Additionally, consider the need for features such as call recording, which can assist in training staff and monitoring compliance with regulations.

Another critical factor is the need for HIPAA compliance. Patient confidentiality is paramount in healthcare, and any phone system must ensure that it adheres to the regulations set forth by the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA). This includes secure communication channels and data encryption to protect sensitive patient information.

Assessing Technology Options

The technology landscape for phone systems has evolved significantly in recent years, with options ranging from traditional landlines to advanced Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) systems. Each option has its advantages and disadvantages, depending on your medical office's specific requirements.

Traditional landlines may seem reliable, but they often lack the flexibility and scalability that modern healthcare demands. VoIP systems, on the other hand, offer numerous advantages, including lower costs, easy scalability, and a wealth of features such as virtual receptionists, voicemail-to-email, and call forwarding. When selecting a phone system, consider your office's growth potential and choose a system that can easily adapt to changing needs.

Examining Feature Sets

An effective medical office phone system should come equipped with a suite of features designed to streamline operations and enhance patient experience. Some essential features to consider include:

Cost Considerations

While the initial cost of a phone system is an important consideration, it is equally vital to factor in long-term expenses. Traditional systems often come with high installation and maintenance costs, whereas VoIP systems typically offer more predictable pricing structures, with lower upfront investments and affordable monthly fees.

When evaluating costs, consider the total cost of ownership over several years, including setup fees, equipment purchases, maintenance, and any potential upgrades. Additionally, ask about any hidden fees related to call usage, support, or features, as these can significantly impact the overall budget.

Ensuring Reliability and Support

A reliable phone system is essential in a medical office, where communication can be the difference between timely patient care and delays. Investigate the reliability of potential phone systems, including their uptime statistics and any backup systems in place in case of an outage.

Moreover, robust customer support is crucial when selecting a phone system. Choose a provider that offers 24/7 support to ensure that any issues can be resolved quickly, minimizing disruptions to patient care. Read reviews and ask for references from other medical offices to gauge the quality of support and service provided.

Training and Ease of Use

Implementing a new phone system in a medical office requires adequate training for staff to ensure smooth operations. Assess the training resources provided by the phone system vendor. Comprehensive training materials, user manuals, and hands-on training sessions can help staff adapt more quickly to the new system.

Additionally, consider the ease of use of the phone system. A user-friendly interface can reduce the learning curve for staff and allow them to focus more on patient care rather than on navigating complex technology.

Future-Proofing the Investment

Lastly, as technology continues to evolve, it is essential to choose a phone system that can adapt to future needs. Look for vendors that regularly update their software and offer new features as they become available. This will ensure that your medical office remains competitive and continues to meet patient expectations in an ever-changing healthcare environment.

In summary, selecting the right phone system is a critical decision for medical offices seeking to streamline operations and enhance patient care. By understanding the unique needs of a medical office, evaluating technology options, examining feature sets, considering costs, ensuring reliability and support, focusing on training, and future-proofing the investment, medical offices can make an informed choice that ultimately benefits both staff and patients.

title: Meet the AI-powered fan companion: TGR Haas F1 Team RaceMate powered by Infobip
article_text:
New always-on conversational agent with team race intelligence, team insights, and interactive experiences powered by Infobip AgentOS

Global AI-first cloud communications platform Infobip and TGR Haas F1 team are launching 'TGR Haas F1 Team RaceMate powered by Infobip', an AI-powered conversational fan companion on Apple Messages for Business and WhatsApp Business Platform. Always-on and always available, it delivers race intelligence for TGR Haas F1 Team: team race intelligence, grid positions, qualifying outcomes, sprint results, and full session schedules. The AI agent tracks drivers Ollie Bearman and Esteban Ocon with their individual championship standings and performance data in conversational format.

The agent draws from a knowledge base covering driver biographies, team history, and circuit data, adapting to each user's knowledge level. Every interaction begins with a schedule check, ensuring fans always know what's happening right now. It proactively surfaces relevant information, suggests next actions, and maintains conversational flow to keep every interaction effortless and engaging. Delivered in short, succinct messages with the energy of a fellow fan in the grandstand and with smart quick-reply menus, the experience is designed for modern fans seeking instant access to team insights.

Between race weekends, the AI-powered solution keeps fans engaged through gamified experiences: team knowledge quizzes with leaderboards, trivia, and the ability to browse official TGR Haas F1 team merchandise with virtual try-on technology, all without leaving the conversation.

The conversational agent represents the evolution of the Infobip and TGR Haas F1 team partnership announced in Miami last year. Year one proved fan appetite for richer engagement through gamified activations. The foundational infrastructure for year two and beyond enables the partnership's core promise: deeper connections between team and its global fanbase. Built on Infobip's AgentOS technology, it scales to millions of fans while maintaining conversational quality and personalization.

"TGR Haas F1 Team RaceMate powered by Infobip transforms how we connect with our fanbase on their terms," said Michael Heath, Senior Fan Engagement CRM Manager at TGR Haas F1 Team. "An always-on companion that gives fans instant access to the race schedule, team race intelligence, team insights, and personalized experiences. We're not just launching an agent; we're building a deeper relationship with the global community that loves this team."

"With this AI-powered fan companion, we're putting technology in service of genuine relationships and demonstrating the business potential of conversational AI in sports," said Ante Pamukovic, Chief Revenue Officer at Infobip. "It provides what modern fans expect, driving loyalty, expanding fan reach and engagement depth in the digital era."

TGR Haas F1 Team RaceMate powered by Infobip represents a new model for how sports teams can build direct, personalized, always-available relationships with their global fanbase. The fan companion launches during the Miami Grand Prix taking place from 1-3 May 2026 and will be available throughout the 2026 season on WhatsApp Business Platform and Apple Messages for Business.

Read more about the partnership here: https://www.infobip.com/haas-f1-team-partnership

title: Kyivstar, Swarmer, and Ferrexpo become first Ukrainian companies to join Ukraine Reconstruction ETF
article_text:
Kyivstar Group Ltd, the owner of Ukraine's largest telecom operator Kyivstar, defense technology company Swarmer, and mining company Ferrexpo have become the first Ukrainian issuers to be included in the 50-company basket of the Ukraine Reconstruction ETF, which is listed on the London Stock Exchange.

"It's exciting to see the first Ukrainian companies joining the ETF Ukraine Reconstruction UCITS. The ETF now provides exposure to both global companies that have the opportunity to help Ukraine's recovery, as well as Ukrainian companies that will also participate in and benefit from Ukraine's recovery," Hector McNeil, co-founder and co-CEO of HANetf, the British investment firm that launched the fund, commented in a press release.

To facilitate the early admission of Ukrainian companies to the stock exchange, the benchmark VettaFi Ukraine Reconstruction index has lowered its liquidity thresholds - a move that will signal to Ukrainian companies that there is a buyer for their public shares, according to a press release.

"These aren't just numbers or indices; they're a significant step forward. For the first time, an international investment instrument of this caliber is directly investing in Ukrainian businesses. This is about trust. It's about investors' faith in Ukraine's future. It's about how, even in the most challenging times, Ukrainian companies not only survive, but also grow, integrate into global markets, and attract capital," Ukraine's Ambassador to the United Kingdom, Valeriy Zaluzhny, wrote on Telegram.

Additionally, Swarmer was also added to the ETF Drone UCITS (DRON), another HANetf fund focused on drone manufacturers.

The VettaFi website does not yet provide information on the proportion of Ukrainian company shares in the fund.

At the same time, Ferrexpo recently warned of terminating its listing on the LSE due to a delay in the publication of financial statements and a planned additional capitalization of at least $100 million, with a current capitalization of approximately $250 million.

Kyivstar and Swarmer shares are traded on the US Nasdaq stock exchange, where their IPOs took place in August last year and March this year, respectively, with a market capitalization of $2.7 billion and $459.3 million, respectively.

"The ETF targets a diversified portfolio of European infrastructure and defense companies, selected global industrial companies, companies listed on Ukrainian exchanges, and a limited portion of Ukraine-focused investment funds," the fund's presentation noted when it was launched in mid-March of this year.

"At launch, the portfolio consists primarily of well-known European and global listed companies capable of delivering large-scale infrastructure, energy, industrial, and defense projects. These firms are expected to play a central role in reconstruction activities in line with EU-aligned regulatory frameworks," says a press release posted on the LSE website.

As of March, the basket, which is revised quarterly, had the largest weightings of Siemens Energy AG at 5.29%, BAE Systems PLC at 5.20%, Johnson Controls International PLC at 4.55%, ABB Limited at 4.44%, Caterpillar Inc at 4.41%, Vinci SA at 4.27%, Schneider Electric SE at 4.23%, Eaton Corporation PLC at 4.21%, Rheinmetall AG at 4.07% and CRH PLC at 3.86%.

The total market capitalization of all 50 companies was $2.25 trillion. The largest number of companies came from the United States (27.5%), France (16.5%), Switzerland (10.1%), Germany (9.7%), and Sweden (9.4%).

The VettaFi Ukraine Reconstruction index began counting on January 25, 2022, at 1000. On March 13 of this year, its value was 1837.71 points, on April 20 it reached a maximum of 1997.79 points, and on April 29 it was 1982.29 points.

The fund's net assets, according to information on its website, have grown from just over EUR 1 million to EUR 6.51 million since mid-March, and the number of shares has increased from 130,000 to 780,000, while overall assets under HANetf's management amount to approximately $5.33 billion.

Trading in the Ukraine Reconstruction ETF on the London Stock Exchange (LSE) began on March 13 at $9.43 per share, and is currently trading at $9.82.

In addition to the LSE, the fund is also listed on the Deutsche Börse Xetra and Borsa Italiana in Milan.

title: South Africa's telco landscape undergoing quality-led shift
article_text:
South Africa's mobile market is undergoing a structural shift, as challenger operators start to capture high-value subscribers from market leaders.

According to Opensignal's latest subscriber analytics data, the shift is no longer price-led, but rather driven by quality.

South Africa's largest mobile operator Vodacom has lost seven percentage points of market share, from 43% in the first quarter of 2024 to 36% in fourth quarter of 2025.

While MTN absorbed some of that user base, increasing two percentage points from 29% to 31% in the same period, the majority of the subscriber gains are concentrated among challenger operators Telkom and Cell C.

Telkom recorded a three percentage point increase from 16% to 19% and Cell C's market share increased one percentage point from 8% to 9% during the same period.

"This is no longer just prepaid churn at the margins. Incumbents are now exposed in higher-value segments. This disruption is not price-led, but rather quality," Opensignal said in a statement issued on Thursday, which highlighted that the challengers' growth is being driven by improved network quality and targeted commercial execution.

Historically, challengers Telkom and Cell C relied heavily on aggressive prepaid pricing to acquire price-sensitive users, a strategy reflected in their traditionally lower average revenue per user.

"Today, however, that model is evolving. Powered by a rapid quality catch-up, these challengers are increasingly capturing higher-margin, top-tier subscribers who are migrating away from legacy networks."

As consistent quality improves, they are increasingly attracting higher-value subscribers who were previously locked into incumbent networks.

This also emerges as capital is reallocated from network resilience to network optimisation and improving performance as South Africa's electricity grid stabilises.

The organisation explained that persistent load-shedding forced the industry to spend heavily on keeping towers powered, with the Independent Communications Authority indicating that operators spent about R2.6-billion on backup batteries solely in 2023/24.

"For the past five years, competition was defined by survival - who had the capital to keep the towers powered during outages. That chapter is closing. It signals that the market has crossed a threshold into the post-resilience era."

This has led to baseline network reliability for the entire country increasing.

The transition to optimisation is accelerating improvements in network quality, particularly among challenger brands, which are bringing their network experience progressively up to par with market leaders and reducing the historical gap with incumbents.

This removes a key barrier to switching: high-value users no longer need to trade reliability for price, making them more willing to consider alternative providers.

Meanwhile, according to Opensignal's subscriber analytics win/loss composition data shows Vodacom is steadily losing share mainly to MTN, a migration that migration aligns with local network performance and MTN's advantage in consistent quality across markets.

"National averages, however, mask important regional dynamics. In major economic centres like Cape Town and the City of Tshwane, MTN outperforms its competitors in consistent quality. Yet here in these specific metropolitans, MTN currently holds an underweight market share relative to its national average," Opensignal continued, pointing out that the localised superiority is not an accident.

MTN's aggressive R1.5-billion investment strategy to counter load-shedding and upgrade base station power resilience is now acting as a primary commercial lever.

"By delivering a measurably higher consistent quality in these specific regional pockets, MTN is effectively attracting premium, data-centric subscribers, particularly those using high-end devices that unlock superior 5G mobile experiences, who value reliability above all else.

"Telkom and Cell C present a highly effective value-quality intersection strategy. While they may still sit slightly lower on the overall consistent quality matrix compared to MTN, their networks have crossed the threshold of good enough for the modern high-value consumers."

Opensignal's previous analysis shows that between May 2023 and the end of 2024, Cell C's consistent quality increased by 15 percentage points, while Telkom saw steady, sustained gains to push its network parity forward - momentum that is continuing according to more recent consistent quality data.

By transitioning to a virtual RAN model operating on MTN's infrastructure, Cell C instantly elevated its network.

"Rather than fighting a capital expenditure (capex) war, they are using this improved, outsourced consistent quality to offer exceptional digital experiences and aggressive data value."

Cell C is also leveraging this reliable baseline to host mobile virtual network operators partners, such as Capitec

Telkom is driving active user growth through lower prices, as well as aggressive network and regional expansion.

"For example, a regional expansion strategy targeting under-indexed, non-metropolitan areas has gained massive traction, with Telkom reporting a 5.6% increase in its share of subscriber acquisitions in those regions," Opensignal explained.

"Paired with tailored data platforms such as Mo'Nice and Mo'Town and R5.8-billion in capex that delivered a core network availability of 99.99% and a net promoter score of 72.3, Telekom is offering a friction-free experience that attracts switchers across all demographics."

By achieving this baseline of reliable consistent quality, challengers opened the door to acquiring high-value users who would not have previously considered switching from the market leader.

"This multiyear quality catch-up laid the foundation for the subscriber migration we are witnessing in the market today."

title: Henry Ford College student selected for Cisco Dream Team
article_text:
Add Yahoo as a preferred source to see more of our stories on Google.

Detroit student Jasmine Hardy is heading to Las Vegas as part of the Cisco Networking Academy U.S. Dream Team.

Hardy, a student at Henry Ford College, is the only student from Michigan and one of 10 from North America selected for the team, according to a community announcement. She will attend CiscoLive! in Las Vegas from May 27 to June 5.

"It feels amazing and unreal! It's definitely a proud moment for me," Hardy said in the announcement.

Hardy is first woman from Henry Ford College selected

Hardy is the third student and the first woman from HFC to be selected for the Dream Team, according to the announcement. Kenith Lewis III was selected in 2015, and Garrett McCollum was selected in 2012.

"This is a huge achievement for both Jasmine and HFC. Congratulations to Jasmine," Lewis said in the announcement.

More than 100 students from the United States and Canada applied for the Dream Team's 10 spots. Final selections were based on geographic representation, diversity, technical and professional skills, instructor recommendations and impact outside the classroom, according to the announcement.

Dream Team will work hands-on at CiscoLive!

Two instructors will join the 10 students at CiscoLive!, which is Cisco Systems Inc.'s largest customer event. The students will have the opportunity to work hands-on alongside industry leaders, including IT professionals and engineers, to deploy and support the event's Network Operations Center (NOC), as well as support Cisco customers at the Help Desk.

The students will gain real-world experience, exposure to industry professionals and learn about the latest technologies. Now in its 14th year, the Dream Team has become a vital element to the success of CiscoLive!, according to the announcement.

Hardy is working toward associate degree in electrical engineering technology

Hardy is an alumna of Woodhaven High School in Brownstown Township. She is working toward her associate degree in electrical engineering technology and her Cisco certification at HFC, both of which she will earn in 2027, according to the announcement. She also completed coursework at Wayne State University. Her goal is to become an electrical engineer, following in the footsteps of her father, Randolph Hardy, an alumnus of HFC who earned his associate degree in electrical engineering technology.

"That played a huge part in my attending here. I love the environment at HFC. The teachers are wonderful and very attentive," Hardy said in the announcement. "I'm similar to my father; we both share an interest in electronics and computers. I also have an interest in troubleshooting. I like to learn how things work, which drew me to electrical engineering and Cisco."

Hardy is excited about traveling to Las Vegas for CiscoLive!.

"I look forward to learning a lot," she said in the announcement. "It will be a new experience for me, but I'm up for it. It's a new challenge, and I love new challenges!"

Instructors praise Hardy's selection

HFC Cisco instructor Todd Browning said that it came as no surprise that Hardy was selected for the Dream Team.

"Jasmine is an excellent student who is very deserving of this opportunity," Browning said in the announcement. "She continues to impress me with her leadership skills and network troubleshooting skills. I believe Jasmine will be an invaluable asset to the Dream Team!"

HFC Cisco instructor Kathryn Fitzner also praised Hardy.

Subscribe: Follow this story. Subscribe to the Monroe News

"She is the type of student that instructors love to have in class -- motivated, hardworking and talented," Fitzner said in the announcement.
